Class Problem

java.lang.Object
model.Problem
public class Problem extends Object
Describes an instance of the Best Route problem. Manages the collection of locations and routes, ensures data integrity, and provides algorithms to validate the reachability between two endpoints.

  • Field Details

    • locations

      private List<Location> locations

    • routes

      private List<Route> routes

    • visited

      private boolean[] visited

  • Constructor Details

    • Problem

      public Problem()
      Initializes a new Problem instance with empty lists for locations and routes.

  • Method Details

    • getLocations

      public List<Location> getLocations()
      Retrieves an unmodifiable view of the locations registered in the problem.
      Returns:
      A List of Location objects.

    • getRoutes

      public List<Route> getRoutes()
      Retrieves an unmodifiable view of the routes registered in the problem.
      Returns:
      A List of Route objects.

    • addLocation

      public void addLocation(Location newLocation)
      Adds a unique location to the problem instance. Prints an error if it already exists.
      Parameters:
      newLocation - The Location to add.

    • addLocations

      public void addLocations(Location... newLocations)
      Adds multiple unique locations to the problem instance using varargs.
      Parameters:
      newLocations - A variable number of Location objects.

    • addRoute

      public void addRoute(Route newRoute)
      Adds a unique route to the problem instance. Prints an error if it already exists.
      Parameters:
      newRoute - The Route to add.

    • addRoutes

      public void addRoutes(Route... newRoutes)
      Adds multiple unique routes to the problem instance using varargs.
      Parameters:
      newRoutes - A variable number of Route objects.

    • validateProblem

      public boolean validateProblem()
      Validates if the problem has enough data to be solved (at least 2 locations and 1 route).
      Returns:
      true if the problem setup is valid, false otherwise.

    • searchRoute

      public boolean searchRoute(Location currentLocation, Location destination, List<Route> routes)
      Recursively searches for a valid path between two locations using Depth-First Search (DFS). It treats routes as bidirectional paths.
      Parameters:
      currentLocation - The node currently being evaluated.
      destination - The target node we want to reach.
      routes - The list of available routes connecting locations.
      Returns:
      true if a valid continuous path exists, false otherwise.

    • validateRoute

      public boolean validateRoute(Location start, Location end)
      Initiates the reachability check between two specified locations. It first validates the problem variables and initializes the tracking array.
      Parameters:
      start - The starting Location.
      end - The target destination Location.
      Returns:
      true if a path exists and is found, false otherwise.
